**Progress Level 7: Gravity Age**

| Weapon         | Avail | Cost  | Dur | Pow | Acc | Range (S/M/L) | Type    | Damage (O/G/A)                  | Actions |
|---------------|-------|-------|-----|-----|-----|---------------|---------|----------------------------------|---------|
| Mass cannon   | Com   | 300K  | 3   | 3   | —   | 5/10/15       | LI (p)  | d6+1s/d4+2w/d4m                  | —       |
| Mass converter| Res   | 1M    | 4   | 4   | —   | 3/6/9         | En (e)  | d6+2s/d6+1w/d6+3w               | —       |
| Matter torpedo| Res   | 600K  | 3   | 2   | —   | 2/5/10        | En (e)  | d6+3s/d6+3w/d6+2m               | —       |
| Missile, MRB  | Res   | 200K  | —   | —   | —   | 10/20/30      | En (g)  | 2d6s/2d6w/d6+3m                  | —       |
| Particle beam | Res   | 500K  | 3   | 4   | —   | 6/12/18       | En (e)  | 2d4+1s/2d4w/d8+1m               | —       |
| Plasma cannon | Mil   | 400K  | 3   | 3   | —   | 4/8/16        | En (e)  | d6+3s/d4+1m/d4+3m               | —       |
| Tractor beam  | Com   | 500K  | 2   | *   | —   | 6/9           | En (e)  | d6+2w/d8+2w/d8+1m (See desc.)   | —       |

**Progress Level 8: Energy Age**

| Weapon         | Avail | Cost  | Dur | Pow | Acc | Range (S/M/L) | Type    | Damage (O/G/A)                  | Actions |
|---------------|-------|-------|-----|-----|-----|---------------|---------|----------------------------------|---------|
| EM torpedo    | Res   | 450K  | —   | —   | —   | 5/10/20       | En (g)  | d6+3s/2d8s/d4+8w                | —       |
| Kinetic lance | Mil   | 750K  | 3   | 4   | —   | 6/12/20       | HI (e)  | d4+1w/2d4w/d4+3m               | —       |
| Maser cannon  | Com   | 400K  | 2   | 3   | —   | —             | En (e)  | d6+2w/d4+1m/d8+2m               | —       |

entries.title: Measurements
entries.content:
Key measurements for spaceship operations:
- **Mm** = Megameter = 1,000 km = 1 million meters
- **Mpp** = Megameters per phase = 1 million kilometers per hour
- **AU** = Astronomical Unit = 150 million km (Earth-Sun distance)
- **c** = speed of light = approximately 300,000 km/s = about 8 AU/hour

entries.title: System Removal Cost and Time
entries.content:
Cost and time to remove spaceship systems for customization.

| System    | Removal Cost (% of original) | Time (weeks/durability) |
|-----------|------------------------------|-------------------------|
| Cargo     | 50%                          | 1 wk/dur                |
| Comm      | 10%                          | 1 wk/dur                |
| Computer  | 10%                          | 4 wk/dur                |
| Crew      | 10%                          | 1 wk/dur                |
| Defense   | 10%                          | 2 wk/dur                |
| Drive     | 50%                          | 8 wk/dur                |
| Engine    | 50%                          | 6 wk/dur                |
| Power     | 25%                          | 4 wk/dur                |
| Sensor    | 10%                          | 4 wk/dur                |
| Support   | 25%                          | 2 wk/dur                |
| Weapon    | 10%                          | 2 wk/dur                |

Systems without durability cost take 1 week to install. Systems removed may be resold at 10%-80% (d8 x 10) of original price. Damaged systems may be worth only 2%-12% (2d6) of original price as scrap.

entries.title: Stardrive Recharge Time
entries.content:
After making starfall, a driveship needs to accumulate a tachyonic charge before reentering drivespace. Three methods to determine recharge time:
1. Roll d4+1 for number of days.
2. Roll 7d12+36 for number of hours (more precise).
3. Allow engineer to attempt System Operation-engineering skill check to decrease time:
   - Ordinary: 6d12+36 hours
   - Good: 5d12+36 hours
   - Amazing: 4d12+36 hours
   - Rank benefit (4+ ranks in engineering): reduce by additional d12

entries.title: G45: Spaceship Opening Range
entries.content:
Determines opening range of spaceship combat based on conditions and Progress Level.

| Prevailing Condition | PL 6 Range           | PL 7+ Range     |
|---------------------|---------------------|-----------------|
| Hazardous           | 3d6 x 0.1 Mm       | d6 Mm           |
| Crowded             | 6d6 x 0.1 Mm       | 3d6 Mm          |
| Normal              | 6d6+20 x 0.1 Mm    | —               |
| Clear               | 3d6 Mm              | 6d6+20 Mm       |

All measurements are in megameters (Mm).

entries.title: G46: Spaceship Detection Range
entries.content:
Detection ranges for spaceship sensor systems (Ordinary/Good/Amazing skill check results).

| System              | Skill Check (O/G/A)              |
|--------------------|-----------------------------------|
| CE passive array   | 50 Mm/100 Mm/150 Mm              |
| EM detector        | 30 Mm/60 Mm/90 Mm                |
| IR detector        | 2 Mm/4 Mm/6 Mm                   |
| Mass detector      | 10 Mm/50 Mm/100 Mm               |
| Radar, air/space   | 10 Mm/20 Mm/40 Mm                |
| Radar, multiband   | 30 Mm/60 Mm/90 Mm                |
| Radar, multiphase  | 40 Mm/80 Mm/120 Mm               |

entries.title: G47: Space Detection Modifiers
entries.content:
Modifiers to sensor operator's skill check based on obscurity conditions in space.

| Obscurity | Modifier |
|-----------|----------|
| Critical  | +3       |

(Additional rows for lesser obscurity levels apply lesser or no penalties, and good conditions provide bonuses.)

entries.title: Interplanetary Travel Times
entries.content:
Average travel times between points in a star system by Progress Level.

| PL | Average Speed        | Roll             |
|----|---------------------|------------------|
| 5  | 1-3 months          | 10d12 months     |
| 6  | 10 hours            | d20 days         |
| 7  | 1 hour              | 3d12 hours       |
| 8  | 1 hour              | 1d12 hours       |

Average Speed is the vessel's velocity in astronomical units in the given time frame.

entries.title: Spaceship Opening Speeds
entries.content:
**Table G48: Spaceship Opening Speeds**

Opening speeds based on prevailing conditions and Progress Level:

| Condition   | PL 6 Speed        | PL 7+ Speed       |
|------------|-------------------|--------------------|
| Hazardous  | d4 x 0.1 Mpp     | d6 x 0.1 Mpp      |
| Crowded    | d4+2 x 0.1 Mpp   | 2d6 x 0.1 Mpp     |
| Normal     | d6+4 x 0.1 Mpp   | d6 x 1 Mpp        |
| Clear      | 2d6+2 x 0.1 Mpp  | 2d6 x 1 Mpp       |

One Mpp = 1 million kph = 0.001c.

entries.title: G49: Losing Control of a Spaceship
entries.content:
When a pilot fails a Vehicle Operation skill check, a second check determines the outcome.

| Skill Check Result | Outcome        |
|-------------------|----------------|
| Critical Failure  | Major spin     |
| Failure           | Spin           |
| Ordinary          | Yaw            |
| Good              | Under control  |
| Amazing           | Under control  |

**Major spin**: Ship enters spin with +4 penalty on regain attempts; +2 penalty to all crew actions. May crash near large objects.
**Spin**: Ship goes wildly out of control. Roll d8 for final facing. Pilot can't do anything else until control regained. All crew suffer +2 penalty.
**Yaw**: Vehicle slides at an angle (roll d6: 1-3 right, 4-6 left). Continues until pilot's next action.
**Under control**: No trouble; continues on course.

If already in a spin and fails again with any result other than "Under control," enters major spin.

entries.title: Copilot Skills (Spaceship Combat)
entries.content:
The copilot can take over piloting at any time (with +1 penalty from copilot station). Alternatively, the copilot can take an action to assist the pilot's maneuvering. The copilot's Vehicle Operation-space vehicle check determines the modifier to the pilot's next skill check:
- **Critical Failure**: +1 penalty
- **Failure**: no modifier
- **Ordinary**: -1 bonus
- **Good**: -2 bonus
- **Amazing**: -3 bonus

entries.title: Sensor Assistance in Combat
entries.content:
Sensors can assist attacks in two ways:

**1. Improve hit odds**: Sensors operator spends action; skill check provides modifier to weapons operator in next phase:
- Critical Failure: +2 penalty
- Failure: no modifier
- Ordinary: -1 bonus
- Good: -2 bonus
- Amazing: -3 bonus

**2. Target specific compartment**: Sensors operator must have action in same phase as successful shot. Skill check allows adjusting random d20 compartment hit roll:
- Critical Failure: shot misses entirely
- Failure: no modifier
- Ordinary: adjust roll by 1
- Good: adjust roll by 2
- Amazing: adjust roll by 3

**3. Assist defenses**: Sensors operator spends action; modifier applies for rest of current round and all of next round:
- Critical Failure: +2 penalty
- Failure: no modifier
- Ordinary: -1 bonus
- Good: -2 bonus
- Amazing: -3 bonus

entries.title: G50: Compartment Hit Location
entries.content:
When a ship is hit, roll d20 and refer to the column for the ship's number of compartments. The number in that column is the number of the compartment that was hit.

(This is an asymmetric hit location table designed so higher-numbered/outer compartments are hit more often, protecting vital inner systems. Sensor operators can adjust the d20 roll by their skill check result to target specific compartments.)

entries.title: Spaceship Damage and Toughness
entries.content:
All spaceships have Amazing toughness:
- **Good firepower weapons** (most heavy/vehicular weapons): damage degrades one grade (mortal becomes wound, wound becomes stun, stun ignored)
- **Ordinary firepower weapons** (most personal weapons): damage degrades twice (mortal becomes stun, wound and stun ignored)

Example: A 9mm pistol must score an Amazing hit (which inflicts mortal damage, degraded to stun) to affect a ship at all.

entries.title: Command Skills in Spaceship Combat
entries.content:
Commander can use Tactics-space tactics skill check to provide modifier to crew's next action checks:
- **Critical Failure**: +2 penalty for all other crew members
- **Failure**: no modifier
- **Ordinary**: -1 bonus for one other crew member
- **Good**: -1 bonus for all other crew members
- **Amazing**: -2 bonus for all other crew members

entries.title: Communication Skills in Spaceship Combat
entries.content:
Comm officer can code messages. Skill check provides modifier to enemy decoding attempts:
- **Critical Failure**: -2 bonus (helps enemy)
- **Failure**: no modifier
- **Ordinary**: +1 penalty to enemy
- **Good**: +2 penalty
- **Amazing**: +3 penalty

Can also spend action jamming enemy transmissions (modifiers apply to opponent's comms and sensor checks next round).

entries.title: Damage to Passengers and Crew
entries.content:
When a compartment suffers damage, individuals inside suffer the same primary damage reduced one grade. Armor is accounted for first. Damage suffered by passengers and crew is low impact (LI).

Example: Wallace is in a compartment hit for 8 wound damage. Armor absorbs 5, leaving 3 wound damage. Wallace takes 3 stun damage (one grade less).

Characters in completely destroyed compartments may suffer decompression effects.

entries.title: Damage Control Repair Complexities
entries.content:
Full repair complexity requirements:
- **Stun damage**: Ordinary complexity, 3 successes, 1 attempt per round of work
- **Wound damage**: Good complexity, 4 successes; attempts once per minute (if <= half wound rating lost) or once per hour (if > half wound rating lost)
- **Mortal damage**: Amazing complexity, 8 successes + 1 per mortal point; attempts once per day

Juryrigging side effects on full repairs:
- Ordinary juryrig result: 3 additional successes needed
- Good juryrig result: 2 additional successes needed
- Amazing juryrig result: 1 additional success needed

Compartments that have lost all mortal boxes cannot be repaired; must be replaced.

entries.title: Engineering Skills in Spaceship Combat
entries.content:
Engineering officer functions:
1. **Power allocation**: With an action, freely allocate ship's power factors to systems.
2. **Remote repair** (stun damage only): System Operation-engineering skill check:
   - Critical Failure: compartment suffers d4 stun damage
   - Failure: no stun points repaired
   - Ordinary: 1 stun point repaired
   - Good: 2 stun points repaired
   - Amazing: 3 stun points repaired
3. **Assist damage control**: Provides modifier to damage control officer's next repair attempt:
   - Critical Failure: +2 penalty
   - Failure: no modifier
   - Ordinary: -1 bonus
   - Good: -2 bonus
   - Amazing: -3 bonus

entries.title: G51: Alien Artifact Form
entries.content:
Roll d20 to determine the physical form of an alien artifact.

| d20   | Form                               |
|-------|-------------------------------------|
| 1-3   | Appliance or Machine                |
| 4-9   | Carried device (roll d20 sub-table) |
| 10-14 | Clothing/Worn device (roll d20 sub-table) |
| 15    | Graft or Implant (roll d20 sub-table) |
| —     | Procedure or Treatment              |
| —     | Site or Installation (roll d20 sub-table) |
| —     | Vehicle (roll d20 sub-table)        |

**Carried device sub-table (d20):**

| Roll  | Form           |
|-------|----------------|
| 1-2   | Baton          |
| 3     | Book           |
| 4     | Disk           |
| 5     | Case/kit       |
| 6     | Instrument     |
| 7     | Pack           |
| 8     | Pistol         |
| 9     | Rifle          |
| 10    | Shield         |
| 11    | Sphere         |
| 12    | Staff          |
| 13    | Sword          |
| 14    | Tool, small    |
| 15    | Tool, medium   |
| 16-20 | Tool, large    |

**Clothing/Worn device sub-table (d20):**

| Roll  | Form              |
|-------|-------------------|
| 1-2   | Amulet            |
| 3     | Armor             |
| 4     | Bracelet          |
| 5     | Cloak/cape        |
| 6     | Footwear/boots    |
| 7     | Handwear/glove    |
| 8     | Harness/belt      |
| 9     | Headgear/helmet   |
| 10    | Jacket/coat       |
| 11    | Jumpsuit          |
| 12-20 | Ring              |

**Graft or Implant sub-table (d20):**

| Roll  | Form                    |
|-------|-------------------------|
| 1     | Right arm               |
| 2     | Left arm                |
| 3     | Right leg               |
| 4     | Left leg                |
| 5     | Torso                   |
| 6     | Head                    |
| 7     | Full body, skeletal     |
| 8     | Full body, muscular     |
| 9     | Full body, vascular     |
| 10-20 | Full body, nervous      |

**Site or Installation sub-table (d20):**

| Roll   | Form            |
|--------|-----------------|
| 1-10   | Well known      |
| 11-20  | Not well known  |

**Vehicle sub-table (d20):**

| Roll   | Form            |
|--------|-----------------|
| 1-15   | Ground vehicle  |
| 16-18  | Water vehicle   |
| 19-20  | Air vehicle     |
| —      | Space vehicle   |

entries.title: G52: Artifact Purpose
entries.content:
Roll d20 for primary purpose and d20 for secondary purpose.

| d20 (Primary) | d20 (Secondary) | Purpose              |
|---------------|-----------------|----------------------|
| 1-2           | 1-2             | Communication        |
| 3-4           | —               | Control              |
| 5-6           | —               | Defense              |
| 7-8           | —               | Environment          |
| 9-10          | —               | Information          |
| 11-12         | —               | Medical              |
| 13-14         | —               | Mental enhancement   |
| 15-16         | —               | Offense              |
| 17            | —               | Physical enhancement |
| 18            | —               | Transmutation        |
| 19            | —               | Transportation       |
| 20            | —               | None                 |

entries.title: G53: Alien Artifact as Perk
entries.content:
Determines the number of powers and drawbacks for an artifact taken as a perk.

| Primary Powers | Secondary Powers | Drawbacks |
|---------------|-----------------|-----------|
| 1-4           | 0-2             | 0-2       |

Typically: Good or Amazing powers balanced by Slight or Moderate drawbacks.

entries.title: G54: Alien Artifact as Flaw
entries.content:
Determines the number of powers and drawbacks for an artifact taken as a flaw.

| Primary Powers | Secondary Powers | Drawbacks |
|---------------|-----------------|-----------|
| 1-2           | 0-1             | 2-4       |

Typically: Ordinary powers that must be paid for with Moderate or Extreme drawbacks.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Analysis
entries.content:
The artifact provides a detailed analysis of any object or area scanned. Range of 20 meters, affecting one target at a time or an area 6 meters in diameter. Provides -3 bonus to any Science or Investigate skill checks.

- **Ordinary**: All energy forms and relationships analyzed with complete accuracy.
- **Good**: Energy and inanimate matter/compounds analyzed with complete accuracy.
- **Amazing**: Energy, inanimate matter, and living organisms analyzed with complete accuracy.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Anti-life Ray
entries.content:
Beam of energy disrupting bioelectric energy. Fired using Modern Ranged Weapons skill. On hit: d6w/2d4w/3d4w of energy (En) damage at Ordinary firepower. Armor useless against it.

- **Ordinary**: Single target, 20 meters range, once per day.
- **Good**: All targets in 1m wide, 40m long path, three times per day.
- **Amazing**: All targets in 2m wide, 60m long path, six times per day.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Armor
entries.content:
Device can be worn as armor or toughen the wearer's body. If combined with other armor, only the more effective armor is considered.

- **Ordinary**: Absorbs d6+1 hits against any attack.
- **Good**: Absorbs 2d4 hits and provides Good toughness.
- **Amazing**: Absorbs 2d4+1 hits and provides Amazing toughness.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Carrier Wave
entries.content:
Converts user into an energy form for rapid travel. Forms include: electrical (along wires), light/radiation (line of sight), sound/seismic (through water/solids), psionic (all mediums, detectable by Mindwalkers).

If the hero encounters interference (short in wire, smoke, radiation shielding), hero materializes at that point or returns to departure.

- **Ordinary**: Once per day, up to 1 minute.
- **Good**: Four times per day, up to 10 minutes each.
- **Amazing**: Once every 2 hours, up to 15 minutes each.

entries.title: G55: Artifact Powers by Purpose
entries.content:
Roll on the appropriate sub-table (d4) based on the artifact's purpose to determine specific powers.

**Communication (d4):**
1. Cyberconscious
2. Empathy
3. Star transceiver
4. Telepathy

**Control (d4):**
1. Cybercontrol
2. Dark matter control
3. Kinetic control
4. Probability control

**Defense (d4):**
1. Armor
2. Displacement
3. Energy dispersal
4. Kinetic dispersal

**Environmental (d4):**
1. Magnetic control
2. Molecular manipulation
3. Photonic manipulation
4. Stealth field

**Information (d4):**
1. Analysis
2. Omnidata computer
3. Precognition
4. Postcognition

**Medical (d4):**
1. Healing touch
2. Immortality
3. Regenerator
4. Resuscitator

**Mental Enhancement (d4):**
1. Heightened Intelligence
2. Heightened Personality
3. Heightened Will
4. Multitasking

**Offense (d4):**
1. Anti-life ray
2. Dark matter attack
3. Disintegration field
4. Gravity generation

**Physical Enhancement (d4):**
1. Heightened Constitution
2. Heightened Dexterity
3. Heightened Strength
4. Hyper adaptation

**Transmutation (d4):**
1. Elemental conversion
2. Molecular transformation
3. Oxidation/reduction
4. Virtual matter creation

**Transportation (d4):**
1. Carrier wave
2. Dimensional shift
3. Time travel
4. Teleportation

entries.title: Artifact Power: Cyberconscious
entries.content:
Functions as a gridcaster, allowing the hero to project consciousness into computers, networks, and computerized machinery. Uses Knowledge-computer operation skill.

- **Ordinary**: -2 bonus, must be in contact with target computer.
- **Good**: Range 500 meters, -3 step bonus.
- **Amazing**: Range 50 kilometers, -4 step bonus.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Cybercontrol
entries.content:
Hero can exert control over any computer or computerized machinery. Uses Knowledge-computer operation skill check.

Results:
- **Ordinary success**: Device operation interrupted, +2 penalty or temporary failure.
- **Good success**: Device controlled for duration of concentration.
- **Amazing success**: Commands remain in place until someone repairs the device.

Can be used at will with an action. Hero must see the target device.

- **Ordinary**: -2 bonus, 10 meters range.
- **Good**: -3 bonus, 100 meters range.
- **Amazing**: -4 bonus, 1 kilometer range.

entries.title: Artifact Power: Dark Matter Attack
entries.content:
Once per hour, the hero can condense streams of dark matter into bolts or blades. Uses Modern Ranged Weapons skill check.

- **Ordinary**: 2d4s/2d4w/d8m of En/G damage, 100 meters range.
- **Good**: 3d4s/3d4w/2d4m of En/G damage, 200 meters range.
- **Amazing**: (continues on page 171)

entries.title: Artifact Drawbacks List
entries.content:
A sidebar listing all artifact drawback types available for alien artifacts:
- Blackouts
- Compulsory behavior
- Damping field
- Degeneration
- Disruptive consciousness
- Infamous device
- Leech
- Mental instability
- Power spike
- Uncontrolled function

entries.title: G57: Starfaring Campaign Scale
entries.content:
Describes the basic scale choices for starfaring campaigns.

| Size | Diameter | Stars | Map Scale |
|------|----------|-------|-----------|
| Province | 10 LY | 50 | 1 LY/hex |
| Cluster | 100 LY | 5,000 | 5 LY/hex |
| Region | 1,000 LY | 500,000 | 50 LY/hex |
| Arm | 10,000 LY | 50,000,000 | 500 LY/hex |
| Galaxy | 100,000 LY | 5,000,000,000 | -- |

entries.title: G58: Stars in a System
entries.content:
Roll d20 to determine the number of stars in a particular system.
(Referenced at line 29568, specific die roll breakdowns correspond to single, binary, trinary, etc. systems. The text states: "To determine the number of stars in a particular system, roll d20 and refer to TABLE G58: STARS IN A SYSTEM.")

entries.title: G59: Single-Star Systems (Spectral Class)
entries.content:
Lists stellar spectral classes for single-star systems, with columns for spectral class, color, and number of planets (die roll).

| Spectral Class | Color | Planets |
|---|---|---|
| O | Blue-white | d12-9 |
| B | Blue | d12-9 |
| A | Green | d12-8 |
| F | Yellow | d12-7 |
| G | Yellow | d12-7 |
| K | Orange | d12-7 |
| M | Red | d12-6 |
| Non-main sequence stars: | | |
| Black hole | -- | d12-7 |
| Neutron star | -- | d12-8 |
| White dwarf | -- | d12-7 |
| Black dwarf | -- | -- |
| Brown dwarf | -- | -- |
| Red supergiant | -- | -- |

Also references which Orbit Track type (I, II, etc.) to use on Table G61.

entries.title: G60: Multiple-Star Systems
entries.content:
Lists primary/secondary star combinations for binary and multiple-star systems.

| Primary (Secondary) | Planets |
|---|---|
| O or B (white dwarf) | -- |
| B or A (M) | -- |
| F (G) | -- |
| F (K or M) | -- |
| F (brown dwarf) | -- |
| G (G) | -- |
| G (K or M) | -- |
| G (brown dwarf) | -- |
| K (K or M) | -- |
| K (brown dwarf) | -- |

Notes: For systems with more than two stars, generate a binary pair and a single star (3 stars), two binary pairs (4 stars), two binaries and a single (5 stars), or three binary pairs (6 stars). The number listed is the number of planets in the system. If a roll produces the highest possible number, roll again and add the second result to the first (only reroll once). On the first roll, any result of 0 or less indicates the absence of planets. Also references which column on Table G61: Orbit Tracks to use.

entries.title: G61: Orbit Tracks
entries.content:
Lists orbital rings, distances from the primary star (in AU), and the die roll to determine planet type.

| Ring | Distance (AU) | Planet Type (die roll) |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | 0.3 | d4+3 |
| 2 | 0.4 | d4+4 |
| 3 | 0.7 | d6+4 |
| 4 | 1.0 | -- |
| 5 | 1.5 | 2d6+6 |
| 6 | 2.0 | 2d6+6 |
| 7 | 3.0 | 2d6+6 |
| 8 | 5.0 | 2d6+10 |
| 9 | 10.0 | d8+10 |
| 10 | 15.0 | d8+10 |
| 11 | 20.0 | d6+12 |
| 12 | 30.0 | d6+12 |
| 13 | 40.0 | d8+12 |
| 14 | 50.0 | d8+12 |
| 15 | 55.0 | -- |
| 16 | 70.0 | -- |

Notes: Ring = an orbital track on the System Record Form. The distance is in astronomical units (AU). Planet Type = roll the die indicated and refer to Table G62: Planets to determine the planet's type.

entries.title: G62: Planets
entries.content:
Determines planet type, number of moons, and moon modifier from a die result.

| Die Result | Type | Moons | Moon Modifier |
|---|---|---|---|
| -- | Ring system | None | N/A |
| 1 | Super-Terran, hot | d6-3 | -5 |
| 2 | Sub-Terran | d6-3 | -5 |
| 3 | Terran, hot | d6-4 | -3 |
| 4 | Terran, temperate | d6-2 | -3 |
| 5 | Sub-Terran, temperate | d6-3 | -- |
| 6 | Super-Terran, temperate | d8-3 | -- |
| 7 | Asteroid belt | None | -- |
| 8 | Gas giant | 2d6+2 | -- |
| 9 | Gas giant, small | d12 | -- |
| 10 | Super-Terran, cold | d6 | -- |
| 11 | Terran, cold | d4 | -- |
| 12 | Sub-Terran, cold | d3 | -- |
| 13+ | Comet belt | -- | -- |

Notes: Type defines the planetary body. Moons indicates the number of moons the body possesses. A result less than 1 indicates an absence of moons. For each moon that exists, roll once on Table G63: Moons. Moon Modifier lists a modifier that applies to the 2d6 roll made on Table G63.

entries.title: G63: Moons
entries.content:
Roll 2d6 (plus Moon Modifier from Table G62) to determine the type of each moon.

| 2d6 Result | Type |
|---|---|
| 2 or less | Tiny |
| 3-4 | Small |
| 5-7 | Ring system |
| 8-9 | Sub-Terran |
| 10-11 | Terran |
| 12+ | Super-Terran |

Notes: A moon has the same temperature as its parent planet. Consider gas giants to be cold -- but see the text for an exception (large moons of gas giants orbiting close to their star may be temperate).

entries.title: G64: Planetary Environments
entries.content:
A large table for determining planetary environment class and GRAPH ratings. Roll varies by planet size: for Terran planets and small gas giants, roll d20; for sub-Terrans, roll d12; for super-Terrans, roll d12+8; for large gas giants, roll d12+5.

**Temperate worlds:**

| Roll | Class | GRAPH | Life | Ocean |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Class 4 | G1/R4/A0/P0/H0 | -- | -- |
| 2 | Class 3 | G1/R2/A1/P1/H4 | -- | -- |
| 3 | Class 3 | G1/R2/A4/P2/H1 | -- | -- |
| 4 | Class 3 | G1/R1/A1/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 5 | Class 3 | G1/R2/A1/P2/H1 | -- | -- |
| 6 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A1/P2/H3 | -- | -- |
| 7 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A1/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 8 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A1/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 9 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A2/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 10 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A2/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 11 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A2/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 12 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A2/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 13 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A1/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 14 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A1/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 15-16 | Class 2 | G1/R2/A1/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 17+ | Class 3 | G1/R2/A1/P1/H2 | -- | -- |

**Hot worlds:**

| Roll | Class | GRAPH | Life | Ocean |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1-3 | Class 5 | G5/R1/A1/P5/H0 | -- | -- |
| 4 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A1/P4/H0 | -- | -- |
| 5 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A1/P5/H0 | -- | -- |
| 6 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A1/P4/H0 | -- | -- |
| 7 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A1/P4/H0 | -- | -- |
| 8 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A2/P4/H1 | -- | -- |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

**Cold worlds:**

| Roll | Class | GRAPH | Life | Ocean |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| 1 | Class 4 | G1/R1/A0/P0/H5 | -- | -- |
| 2 | Class 3 | G1/R1/A1/P1/H5 | -- | -- |
| 3 | Class 2 | G1/R2/A1/P1/H4 | -- | -- |
| 4 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A1/P2/H4 | -- | -- |
| 5 | Class 2 | G1/R2/A1/P1/H1 | -- | -- |
| 6 | Class 1 | G2/R1/A1/P1/H1 | I | -- |
| 7 | Class 2 | G2/R2/A2/P1/H2 | -- | -- |
| 8 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A2/P4/H4 | -- | -- |
| 9 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A1/P1/H4 | -- | -- |
| 10 | Class 3 | G1/R3/A0/P4/H5 | -- | -- |
| 11 | Class 3 | G4/R1/A0/P5/H5 | -- | -- |
| 12 | Class 2 | G1/R1/A0/P0/H0 | -- | -- |
| ... | ... | ... | ... | ... |

Notes: "Life" column -- if a planet is capable of supporting native life, an entry here lists the possible type(s). See Table G68: Lifeforms. "Ocean" column -- if a planet can have oceans, a roll of d8 that falls within the given range indicates oceans are present. See Table G65: Oceans.

entries.title: G65: Oceans
entries.content:
Roll d8 and cross-reference with planet environment class to determine extent of oceans.

| d8 Roll | Class 1 | Class 2 | Class 3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1-2 | Sparse | None | None |
| 3-4 | Moderate | Sparse | None |
| 5-6 | Abundant | Moderate | Sparse |
| 7-8 | Complete | Abundant | Moderate |

Ocean types:
- Sparse: small landlocked seas or frozen ice caps, covering about one-quarter of the planet.
- Moderate: large landlocked seas or extensive ice caps, covering roughly half the planet.
- Abundant: cover most of the planet's surface, dividing continents from each other.
- Complete: cover the planet, with only a few islands rising above the waves.

entries.title: G66: Climate
entries.content:
Roll d8 and cross-reference with planet environment class to determine climate severity.

| d8 Roll | Class 1 | Class 2 | Class 3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1-2 | Calm | Calm | Active |
| 3-5 | Active | Active | Turbulent |
| 6-7 | Turbulent | Turbulent | Violent |
| 8+ | Violent | Violent | Violent |

Notes: Add 1 to the die roll if the planet has sparse oceans or no oceans, and add an additional 3 if the planet is a gas giant. If the planet has an extreme axial tilt or a fast rotation rate, add 1 for each. If the planet has a mild axial tilt or a slow rotation rate, subtract 1 for each.

Climate types:
- Calm: placid by Earth standards, little variation, infrequent storms, gentle winds.
- Active: similar to Earth -- distinct seasons, intense winds and storms, surface temperature fluctuates by as much as 100 degrees C.
- Turbulent: wider range of temperatures, more powerful storms and winds than active. Mars is an example.
- Violent: drastic temperature range, constant storms, destructive winds. Saturn and Neptune are examples.

entries.title: G67: Landforms
entries.content:
Roll d8 and cross-reference with planet environment class to determine landform profile.

| d8 Roll | Class 1 | Class 2 | Class 3 |
|---|---|---|---|
| 1-3 | Smooth | Smooth | Smooth |
| 4-5 | Varied | Smooth | Smooth |
| 6-7 | Varied | Varied | Rugged |
| 8+ | Rugged | Rugged | Perilous |

Notes: Disregard landforms for gas giants (no land). If the planet has one or more moons or is a sub-Terran world, add 1 to the die roll. If the planet is a super-Terran, or has a corrosive (A4) or super corrosive (A5) atmosphere, subtract 1.

Landform types:
- Smooth: geological landscape less pronounced than Earth's. Low flat mountains, shallow seas, little volcanic activity. Venus is an example.
- Varied: about the same range as Earth's, with active plate tectonics and moderate volcanism.
- Rugged: significantly more exaggerated than Earth's. Higher and sheerer mountains. Mars is an example.
- Perilous: even more pronounced than rugged. Very light gravity or extreme tidal forces may seriously deform a planet's crust.

entries.title: G68: Lifeforms
entries.content:
Determines the biochemical series of life that might exist on a planet. Roll d8; if the result falls within the given range, life of that series exists.

| Series | Breathes | Odds (d8) |
|---|---|---|
| I | Carbon / Oxygen | 1-2 |
| II | Carbon / Hydrogen | 1 |
| III | Silicone / Chlorine | 1 |
| IV | Carbon / Sulfur trioxide | 1-2 |
| V | Carbon / Oxygen | 1 |
| VI | Carbon / Sulfur dioxide | 1 |

Notes: If a planet is capable of supporting native life, the "Life" column on Table G64 lists one or two series. Then roll d8 on this table. If life exists, there is a 75% chance (1-6 on d8) that it is too simple (primordial) to be anything more than scientifically interesting.

entries.title: Organizing Lifeforms -- Five Categories
entries.content:
When heroes explore a new planet, describe lifeforms that fall into one of five general categories:
1. Aggressive carnivores or scavengers.
2. Aggressive herbivores that actively defend themselves from perceived threats.
3. Plants and animals with passive defense mechanisms that may prove dangerous.
4. Pests, vermin, or parasites that may attack through blind instinct.
5. Creatures of scientific interest or with unusual properties.

If a creature isn't going to attack the heroes and doesn't possess anything useful, you don't have to spend time detailing it.

entries.title: Civilization Level (CL 0-8)
entries.content:
Civilization Level is a rough measurement of the extent to which a world has been settled.

| CL | Name | Population | Resource Modifier |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Uninhabited | None | Auto-fail |
| 1 | Unique | ~1,000 | +3 penalty |
| 2 | Scattered | ~10,000 | +2 penalty |
| 3 | City | 100,000+ | +1 penalty |
| 4 | Nation | 1-10 million | No modifier |
| 5 | Continent | ~100 million | -1 bonus |
| 6 | World | ~1 billion | -2 bonus |
| 7 | Improved World | ~10 billion | -3 bonus |
| 8 | Megapolitan World | 25-50 billion | -4 bonus |

entries.title: Law Level (LL 0-8)
entries.content:
Law Level describes a civilization's government in terms of organization and power. Each level has a modifier that applies when heroes attempt illegal acts.

| LL | Name | Modifier | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| 0 | Anarchy | -4 | No law exists except the law of the jungle. |
| 1 | Tribal | -3 | No universal rules of conduct; local groups may develop codes. |
| 2 | Feudal | -2 | Law serves individuals in power. Corruption is rife. |
| 3 | Civic | -1 | Basic standard of conduct, elementary civil rights, some police. |
| 4 | State | 0 | Common laws and enforcement agencies. Corruption is modest. |
| 5 | National | +1 | Like the United States of the 1990s. Specialized law organizations. |
| 6 | Totalitarian | +2 | Authorities may use or disregard civil rights as they see fit. |
| 7 | Technocracy | +3 | Extensive surveillance, propaganda, and mind control. |
| 8 | Nonhuman | +4 | Inhabitants incapable of crime by design or nature. |

Notes: Reverse the modifier from a bonus to a penalty (or vice versa) if the heroes try to make use of a local law agency. A large civilization (CL 3 or higher) must have a Law Level equal to or greater than its Civilization Level or else the society falls into anarchy.

entries.title: Adventure Design Template
entries.content:
Summary outline for spontaneous or planned adventure design:
- Idea
- Background
- Supporting Cast
- The Trigger

entries.title: Three-Act Adventure Structure
entries.content:
The basic structure of a three-act adventure:

**Act One**
- Scene One: Trigger
- Scene Two: Complications
- Scene Three: Minor Resolution and Dramatic High Note

**Act Two**
- Scene One: Trigger
- Scene Two: Complications
- Scene Three: Minor Resolution and Dramatic High Note

**Act Three**
- Scene One: Trigger
- Scene Two: Complications
- Scene Three: Major Resolution and Climax

Alternates: Plan for two or three alternate paths that you can eventually use to get back to the main story.
- Alternate #1
- Alternate #2

entries.title: Adventure Hook #1: Hijacked!
entries.content: Modern-day to near-future technothriller. Terrorists take control of a high-speed passenger train with the President aboard. Heroes must neutralize terrorists, save the President, and regain control before the train reaches the coast -- where the terrorists plan to detonate a nuclear device. Includes background, supporting cast notes, and a trigger scene.

entries.title: Adventure Hook #2: Installation 251
entries.content: Contemporary horror SF tale. In a secret Alaskan government facility, an alien creature has escaped captivity and is systematically killing personnel. Heroes are sent to investigate. Includes background, supporting cast notes, and a trigger scene in which the base goes into quarantine lockdown.

entries.title: Adventure Hook #3: Murder on Asteroid 36
entries.content: Near-future PL 6 adventure. A mining detail has uncovered an alien artifact on Asteroid 36 that possesses a malevolent aura driving miners insane and turning some into psychopathic murderers. Heroes must subdue the murderers and secure the artifact. Includes background, supporting cast notes, and a trigger scene.

entries.title: Adventure Hook #4: First Contact
entries.content: Exploration or space opera campaign at PL 7+. The heroes' starship encounters an alien communication signal from an unknown species. Two alien ships -- one being chased and fired upon by another -- crash on a habitable planet. Heroes must investigate. Includes background, supporting cast notes, and a trigger scene.

entries.title: Mutants and Aliens (Optional Rule Note)
entries.content: The mutation system presented in the Player's Handbook does not allow members of an alien species to play mutants. There's no reason you can't choose to allow certain alien races to indulge in genetic experimentation or random mutagenics. The factor you'll want to be aware of is the possibility of a weren hero with a Strength score of 18, or a fraal with an Intelligence score of 18.

entries.title: Four Options for Mutants in the Campaign
entries.content:
1. Mutant heroes don't exist. Mutations are usually harmful and often fatal.
2. Mutant powers are incredibly rare. Only a select few have access. Objects of scorn and scrutiny.
3. Mutants are simply uncommon. Most people know they exist. Social authorities have rules to accommodate them.
4. Everyone is a mutant (extreme option). Result of massive irradiation or alien tinkering.

entries.title: Four Options for Psionics in the Campaign
entries.content:
1. Psionic powers don't exist. ESP, clairvoyance, and telekinesis are hoaxes.
2. Psionic powers available only to a very limited or select group (rare psi-gene).
3. Psionic talents are uncommon but not unheard of. Man on the street knows about them.
4. Everyone possesses psionic powers. Mindwalker skills become Open skills.

entries.title: Three Options for Cybernetics in the Campaign
entries.content:
1. The technology doesn't exist. Neural interface technology has yet to be invented.
2. The technology exists but its use is morally questionable or requires exorbitant resources.
3. The technology exists and is widely available. Minor stylistic cybertech improvements are common.

entries.title: Three Options for Artificial Intelligence
entries.content:
1. AI is impossible. Computers resemble 20th century machines (faster but not sentient).
2. AI is possible but impractical. Built for special purposes, carefully controlled.
3. AIs are relatively common. Everyone has access to computers with decision-making abilities.

entries.title: FX Broad Skill Types
entries.content:
The three FX broad skills:
- Arcane Magic
- Faith
- Super Power

entries.title: G69: FX Broad Skill Costs
entries.content: (Referenced in text.) Standard costs for the FX broad skills: Arcane Magic, Faith, and Super Power. The Gamemaster is free to modify the costs to fit the campaign.

entries.title: G70: Faith FX Specialty Skill Costs
entries.content:
Fixed costs for Faith FX specialty skills:
- Ordinary miracles: 5 skill points
- Good miracles: 10 skill points
- Amazing miracles: 15 skill points

entries.title: G71: Arcane Magic & Super Power FX Specialty Skill Costs
entries.content:
Add up varying effects to get a base cost for the FX specialty skill.

| FX Specialty Skill Effect | Cost |
|---|---|
| Ability Score boost (+1/+3/+5) | 5/10/15 |
| Bonus steps (-1/-2/-3) | 3/5/7 |
| Penalty steps (+1/+2/+3) | 3/5/7 |
| Bonus to damage (1/2/3 points) | 2/4/6 |
| Bonus to combat movement | 5/10/15 |
| Fatigue damage (1-4 points) | -- |
| Stun damage (1-5 points) | -- |
| Stun damage (6-10 points) | -- |
| Stun damage (11-15 points) | -- |
| Wound damage (1-5 points) | -- |
| Wound damage (6-10 points) | -- |
| Wound damage (11-15 points) | -- |
| Mortal damage (1-2 points) | -- |
| Mortal damage (3-5 points) | -- |
| Damage type (O/G/A) | -- |
| Low impact (LI) attack | -- |
| High impact (HI) attack | -- |
| Energy (En) attack | -- |
| Duration, instant | -- |
| Duration (1/2/3 time units) | -- |
| Flight or other special movement | -- |
| Knowledge | -- |
| Range, personal | -- |
| Range, 2/4/6 meters | -- |
| Range, 10/20/30 meters | -- |
| Range, 100/200/300 meters | -- |
| Range, 1/2/3 kilometers | -- |
| Travel (1 km/10 km/100 km) | -- |

Notes: FX specialty skills with a total base cost of 5 points or less are Ordinary quality; 6-10 points = Good quality; 11-15 points = Amazing quality. No FX specialty skill can have a base cost of more than 15 points. The quality determines its cost in FX energy to use.

entries.title: G72: FX Energy
entries.content:
FX Energy available by campaign tone.

| Campaign Tone | Starting | Max | Cost (per point) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Realistic | 5 | 10 | 20 |
| Heroic | 10 | 20 | -- |
| Super Heroic | 15 | 30 | -- |

Notes: Starting = the amount of FX energy a starting character with FX powers begins with. Max = the most FX energy a character can ever have. Cost = the skill points needed to purchase an extra point of energy.

entries.title: G73: FX Energy Costs
entries.content:
FX energy consumed per use of an FX specialty skill, by FX type and outcome.

**Arcane Magic:**

| Outcome | Energy Cost |
|---|---|
| Critical Failure | 3 |
| Failure | 2 |
| Ordinary spell | 1 |
| Good spell | 2 |
| Amazing spell | 3 |

**Faith:**

| Outcome | Energy Cost |
|---|---|
| Critical Failure | 3 |
| Failure | 2 |
| Ordinary miracle | 1 |
| Good miracle | 2 |
| Amazing miracle | 3 |

**Super Power:**

| Outcome | Energy Cost |
|---|---|
| Critical Failure | 3 |
| Failure | 2 |
| Ordinary power | 1 |
| Good power | 2 |
| Amazing power | 3 |

entries.title: FX Examples -- Fate Casting
entries.content:
Arcane Magic, Augur spell: WIL; Good quality, base cost 6
This spell determines the possible fate of a character within the next 24 hours (6 pts); provides general information (3 pts); and requires the casting of runestones (-1 pt) over the course of a skill check of Good complexity (-2 pts).

entries.title: FX Examples -- Bolt of Lightning
entries.content:
Arcane Magic, Conjure spell: WIL; Good quality, base cost 8
This spell aims a bolt of lightning at a target (energy attack, 3 pts). It inflicts Ordinary damage (0 pts) of d4s/d4w/d4+2w (low stun 1 pt; low wound 2 pts). The bolt's range is 2/4/6 meters (2 pts). Trappings: wood shavings from a lightning-struck tree (-1 pt) and a skill check of Good complexity (-2 pts).

entries.title: FX Examples -- Fly
entries.content:
Arcane Magic, Summon spell: WIL; Good quality, base cost 9
This spell allows the caster to fly (6 pts) at a rate equal to his fly movement rate (see Table P8: Combat Movement Rates in the Player's Handbook). The flight lasts for 2 minutes (4 pts). Requires a component of soap bubbles (-1 pt) and a simple skill check.

entries.title: FX Examples -- Enchant Weapon
entries.content:
Arcane Magic, Transform spell: PER; Amazing quality, base cost 11
This spell temporarily bestows magical qualities upon an ordinary weapon. It provides a 3-point bonus to damage inflicted by the weapon (6 pts), and the weapon remains enchanted for 3 minutes (6 pts). Casting requires a component of silver dust sprinkled over the item (-1 pt) and a simple skill check.

entries.title: FX Examples -- Divine Guidance
entries.content:
Faith, Ordinary miracle: WIL; Ordinary quality, base cost 5
The character prays for advice concerning a particular course of action. The answer comes in the form of a feeling or sense of the rightness or wrongness of an action. The guidance is equivalent to general knowledge.

entries.title: FX Examples -- Divine Blessing
entries.content:
Faith, Good miracle: WIL; Good quality, base cost 10
When this prayer is answered, the miracle worker causes a divine agency to bestow a blessing: -1 bonus to the group's action checks and +1 to the group's resistance modifiers. The blessing remains in effect for 2 rounds.

entries.title: FX Examples -- Song of the Eagle
entries.content:
Faith, Amazing miracle: WIL; Amazing quality, base cost 15
This prayer is answered by changing the miracle worker into an eagle for 1 hour. As an eagle, the character retains his own Intelligence and memories. Durability rating of 6/6/3. Once knocked unconscious or out of mortal points, the character returns to normal form; damage suffered in eagle form remains.

entries.title: FX Examples -- Cloak of Invisibility
entries.content:
Super Power, Relic: DEX; Amazing quality, base cost 12
When the word of power is spoken, the cloak turns its wearer invisible. Provides a -3 bonus (7 pts) to all Stealth checks and adds +2 to resistance modifiers for any attacks requiring sight of the target (5 pts).

entries.title: FX Examples -- Strength of the Behemoth
entries.content:
Super Power, Extreme Ability: CON; Good quality, base cost 8
This power increases the character's Strength score by as much as 3 (10 pts) for as long as the FX energy lasts. Every round uses 2 FX energy points. Degree of success indicates actual Strength increase (+1/+2/+3). Limitation: in the cold (0 degrees C or less), the check is made with a +1 penalty (-1 pt).

entries.title: FX Examples -- Web Spinneret
entries.content:
Super Power, Overscience gadget: DEX; Ordinary quality, base cost 2
An overscience device worn around the character's wrist. Fires a line of superstrong, ultrathin material that sticks to a distant object. Range 10/20/30 meters (4 pts), with +2 and +3 penalty for medium and long range. Webline has effective Strength score of 16. Requires Technical Science-repair skill check of Good complexity to replenish (-2 pts).

entries.title: Animal & Alien Statistics Explanation
entries.content:
Explains how animal and alien creature statistics work:
- Ability Scores: a single value for each, with a range in parentheses for individualization (e.g., STR 16 (d6+12)).
- Intelligence and Personality use an "Animal" scale (e.g., "INT 3 (Animal 11 or d4+9)") -- the Animal value is the effective score for animal-appropriate tasks.
- Attacks and Defenses: use +d0 base situation die. Predators have higher attack scores.
- Skills: broad skills with scores in brackets, specialty skills likewise (e.g., "Athletics [16]-climb [18]").

entries.title: Animals Combat Behavior
entries.content: Animals don't attack just because a human crosses their path. They attack because they're frightened, angered, startled, or (rarely) hungry. Unless actively hunting, animals engage in warning displays (growling, bluffing a charge, beating its chest). If an animal does stalk a hero, it uses Stealth to surprise. Large animals often try to knock targets flat first. An animal with several attack options can try one of each as a single action. Few animals fight to the death.

Notes: Eight-legged insectoid. Four front legs attack simultaneously against single target. Two consecutive successful claw attacks = automatic bite in current phase.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Mollusk
entries.content:
STR 13 (d8+10), INT 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4), DEX 11 (d4+9), WIL 7 (d4+5), CON 12 (d4+10), PER 1 (Animal 4 or d4+2)
Durability: 12/12/6/6 | Action check: 13+/12/6/3 | Move: walk 10, swim 50 | #Actions: 2 | Reaction score: Ordinary/1
Attacks: Tentacles (x4) 14/7/4 d4s/d6+1s/d4w LI/O; Bite 14/7/4 d4w/d4+2w/d6+1w LI/O
Defenses: +2 vs melee, +1 vs ranged | Armor: d6+2 (LI), d6+1 (HI), 2d4 (En)
Skills: Stealth [11]-hide [18]; Stamina [12]-endurance [16]; Awareness [7]-intuition [10], perception [8]; Resolve [7]-physical resolve [10].
Notes: Octopus-like with armored mantle/shell. Can drag itself on land up to 5 hours. Uses 4 tentacles at once. Tentacle hit wraps and auto-constricts. Hero can sever tentacle with 5+ wound damage (doesn't count against overall durability). -1 bonus from suckers on opposed Strength check.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Nematode
entries.content:
STR 7 (d4+4), INT 1 (Animal 3 or d4+1), DEX 14 (2d4+9), WIL 9 (d4+6), CON 6 (d4+4), PER 1 (Animal 4 or d4+2)
Durability: 6/6/3/3 | Action check: 12+/11/5/2 | Move: run 24, walk 8 | #Actions: 2 | Reaction score: Ordinary/1
Attacks: Sting 8/4/2 1s/1w/2w LI/O; Blood drain: 1w per minute LI/O
Defenses: no mod vs melee, +2 vs ranged | Armor: none
Skills: Stealth [14]-sneak [16]; Stamina [6]-endurance [10]; Awareness [9]-intuition [10]; Investigate [9]-track [15]; Resolve [9]-physical resolve [12].
Notes: 2-meter blood-sucking worm with paralyzing sting (onset 1 phase). Attacks while victims sleep. Drains 1 wound per 5 rounds, up to 24 wound points. Congregates in nests of 3d8 individuals.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Ophidian
entries.content:
STR 12 (d8+8), INT 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4), DEX 11 (d4+9), WIL 11 (d4+9), CON 8 (d4+6), PER 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4)
Durability: 8/8/4/4 | Action check: 11+/10/5/2 | Move: walk 8 | #Actions: 3 | Reaction score: Ordinary/2
Attacks: Strike 16/8/4 d4+1s/d6+1s/d4w LI/O; Constrict 14/7/4 d4+2w/d6+2w/d4m LI/O
Defenses: +2 vs melee, +1 vs ranged | Armor: d6+1 (LI), d6 (HI), d4 (En)
Skills: Stealth [11]-hide [14]; Stamina [8]-endurance [14]; Awareness [11]; Resolve [11]-physical resolve [14].
Notes: Python-sized snake with exoskeleton and daggerlike bone ribs. Lurks in stream/pond shallows. Embrace can only be broken by killing the creature.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Piscine
entries.content:
STR 9 (d4+7), INT 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4), DEX 14 (d4+12), WIL 7 (d4+5), CON 5 (d4+3), PER 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4)
Durability: 5/5/3/3 | Action check: 14+/13/6/3 | Move: swim 70 | #Actions: 2 | Reaction score: Ordinary/2
Attacks: Bite 10/5/2 d6s/d4w/d4+1w LI/O; Shock 12/6/3 d8s/d8w/d12w LI/O
Defenses: +1 vs melee, +2 vs ranged | Armor: none
Skills: Movement [5]; Stamina [5]-endurance [10]; Awareness [7]; Resolve [7].
Notes: 2-meter barracuda-like fish with electric eel-like shock. Shock delivered through long fin-whips (2 meter reach). Can deliver three shocks per combat scene. Hunts in schools of 5-10.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Primate
entries.content:
STR 15 (d6+11), INT 5 (Animal 8 or 2d4+3), DEX 13 (d6+10), WIL 15 (d4+12), CON 14 (d4+12), PER 5 (Animal 8 or 2d4+3)
Durability: 14/14/7/7 | Action check: 17+/16/8/4 | Move: sprint 50, run 30, walk 10, (jump 12) | #Actions: 3 | Reaction score: Good/2
Attacks: Pummel 16/8/4 d6+1s/d8w/d6+2w LI/O; Bite 14/7/3 d4s/d6+1s/d4w LI/O; Thrown rock 14/7/3 d4-1s/d4s/d4+2s LI/O
Defenses: +3 vs melee, +2 vs ranged | Armor: d4 (LI), d4-1 (HI), d4 (En)
Skills: Athletics [15]-climb [20], jump [18], throw [16]; Acrobatics [13]-fall [14]; Movement [14]; Stamina [14]-endurance [18]; Awareness [15]-intuition [18], perception [18]; Investigate [15]-search [16]; Resolve [15].
Notes: Taller and thinner than a gorilla, 30-40 kg heavier than an adult human. Very social, troops up to 40. Extremely aggressive when defending lair.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Rodent
entries.content:
STR 4 (d4+2), INT 2 (Animal 7 or d4+5), DEX 14 (d4+12), WIL 7 (d4+5), CON 3 (d4+0), PER 4 (Animal 13 or d4+11)
Durability: 3/3/2/2 | Action check: 15+/14/7/2 | Move: sprint 60, run 40, walk 12, (jump 10) | #Actions: 2 | Reaction score: Good/1
Attacks: Dart 16/8/4 1s/2s/1w LI/O plus poison
Defenses: +1 vs melee, +3 vs ranged | Armor: d4-1 (LI), none (HI), d4-1 (En)
Skills: Athletics [4]-jump [10], throw [8]; Acrobatics [14]-fall [16]; Stealth [14]-hide [19]; Stamina [3]; Awareness [7]-intuition [10].
Notes: Jackrabbit-sized omnivore. Defense mechanism: 4 poisoned darts fired from shoulder hump (range 4/8/12 meters). Irritating venom (+2 penalty to Con feat check). Onset 2 rounds, duration 6 hours. Lives in colonies of dozens.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Saurian
entries.content:
STR 20 (2d4+15), INT 3 (Animal 10 or 2d4+5), DEX 8 (d4+6), WIL 10 (d4+8), CON 18 (2d6+11), PER 2 (Animal 7 or d4+5)
Durability: 27/27/13/13 | Action check: 13+/12/6/3 | Move: sprint 50, run 30, walk 16, (jump 16) | #Actions: 3 | Reaction score: Ordinary/2
Attacks: Bite 22/11/5 2d6w/d4+1m/d6+2m LI/G; Claw/kick 22/11/5 d4+3w/d6+4w/d4m LI/G; Tail lash 18/9/5 d8s/d12s/d6w LI/G
Defenses: Good toughness, +3 vs melee, no mod vs ranged | Armor: d6+2 (LI), d4+1 (HI), d6+1 (En)
Skills: Stealth [9]; Movement [18]; Stamina [18]-endurance [22]; Awareness [10]-intuition [14], perception [12]; Investigate [10]-search [12]; Resolve [10]-physical resolve [16].
Notes: Allosaurus-sized predator. Good toughness. Moderately stealthy ambush predator. Extraordinarily fearless and bloodthirsty.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Trilobite
entries.content:
STR 8 (d4+6), INT 1 (Animal 4 or d4+2), DEX 12 (d4+10), WIL 9 (d4+7), CON 6 (d4+4), PER 2 (Animal 6 or d4+4)
Durability: 6/6/3/3 | Move: run 80, walk 10, swim 10 | Reaction score: Marginal/1 | Action check: 9+/8/4/2 | #Actions: 2
Attacks: Bite 10/5/2 d4s/d4w/d4+2w LI/O; Acid jet 10/5/2 d4w/d4+2w/2d4+1w LI/G
Defenses: +1 vs melee, +2 vs ranged | Armor: d4+3 (LI), d4+1 (HI), d4+2 (En)
Skills: Stealth [12]-hide [14]; Stamina [6]-endurance [10]; Awareness [9]-intuition [12]; Resolve [9].
Notes: Armored arthropod over 1 meter long. Acid jet with 6 meter range and Good firepower. Can use acid attack once every 12 hours.

entries.title: Alien Compendium -- Ursine
entries.content:
STR 17 (2d4+12), INT 5 (Animal 14 or d4+12), DEX 11 (d4+9), WIL 14 (d4+12), CON 16 (2d4+11), PER 4 (Animal 13 or d4+11)
Durability: 24/24/12/12 | Action check: 15+/14/7/3 | Move: sprint 60, run 40, walk 10 | #Actions: 4 | Reaction score: Good/2
Attacks: Bite 20/10/5 d4w/d4+2w/d4+4w LI/G; Claws 20/10/5 d8s/d4w/d6+1w LI/G
Defenses: Resistant to toxins (-4 bonus on Con feat checks vs poison), +4 vs melee, +1 vs ranged | Armor: d6+1 (LI), d4+1 (HI), d6 (En)
Skills: Athletics [17]; Movement [16]; Stamina [16]-endurance [20]; Awareness [14]-intuition [16]; Investigate [14]-search [16], track [16]; Resolve [14]-physical resolve [20].
Notes: Bearlike creature with thick loose folds of tough hide. Hunts in twos or threes using ruses, distractions, and cooperative tactics. Virtually immune to most poisons (-4 bonus to Con feat checks).

entries.title: AD&D to Alternity Conversion -- Race/Species Abilities
entries.content:
Conversion rules for AD&D character races to Alternity:

**Dwarves:** Free broad skills: Athletics, Stamina, Knowledge, Awareness, Resolve, Interaction. Magic and Poison Resistance (+2 resistance modifier vs Arcane Magic/Faith FX; -2 bonus to Con feat checks vs poison/radiation). Defensive Skill (+2 vs melee/unarmed from humanoids 3+ meters tall). Infravision. Mining Skills (Will-based broad skill: detect grade/slope -2 step bonus, detect new construction -2 step bonus, detect sliding/shifting walls -1 step bonus, detect stonework traps no modifier, detect depth no modifier).

**Elves:** Free broad skills: Athletics, Melee Weapons or Primitive Ranged Weapons, Stealth, Knowledge, Awareness, Interaction. Charm Resistance (+4 vs charm/sleep FX). Infravision. Secret Door Detection (-2 bonus to Investigate-search).

**Gnomes:** Free broad skills: Athletics, Stamina, Manipulation, Knowledge, Awareness, Interaction. Magic Resistance (+2 vs Arcane Magic/Faith FX). Defensive Skills (+2 vs melee/unarmed from humanoids 2.5+ meters tall). Infravision. Mining Skills (same as Dwarves).

**Halflings:** Free broad skills: Stamina, Stealth, Knowledge, Awareness, Resolve, Interaction. Magic and Poison Resistance (+2 vs Arcane Magic/Faith FX; -2 bonus to Con feat checks vs poison/radiation). Throwing Bonus (-1 bonus to Athletics-throw). Infravision (stout halflings full; mixed halflings negate up to +2 penalties).

**Half-elves:** Free broad skills: Athletics, Stealth, Stamina, Knowledge, Awareness, Interaction. Charm Resistance (+2 vs charm/sleep FX). Infravision. Secret Door Detection (-1 bonus to Investigate-search).

entries.title: AD&D to Alternity Conversion -- Class to Profession
entries.content:
| AD&D Class | Alternity Profession |
|---|---|
| Fighter | Combat Spec |
| Paladin | Combat Spec |
| Ranger | Combat Spec |
| Cleric | Diplomat |
| Druid | Diplomat |
| Thief | Free Agent |
| Bard | Diplomat |
| Mage | Tech Op |
| Specialist Wizard | Tech Op |

entries.title: AD&D Spellcasting Conversion
entries.content:
Conversion rules for spellcasters in a magical universe:
- Wizards, clerics, druids, specialty priests: 7 spell points per level for FX specialty skills.
- Rangers: 5 points per level above 8th.
- Paladins: 5 points per level above 9th.
- These spell points can only be used for FX specialty skills.
- Each AD&D spell must be converted to an FX ability using the FX rules from Chapter 16.
- A 2-point penalty in the AD&D system is roughly equal to a 1-step penalty in the Alternity rules.

entries.title: Magic Missile FX Conversion Example
entries.content:
Magic Missile -- Arcane Magic, Conjure spell: WIL; Ordinary quality, cost 9
Allows caster to manipulate energy into small bolts that inflict Ordinary Energy (En) damage. Skill check determines damage: Critical Failure = random target; Marginal = d4-2w (min 1); Ordinary = d4-1w; Good = d4w; Amazing = d4+1w. Ranges 10/20/30 meters. -1 bonus at short, no modifier at medium, +1 penalty at long. Extra missile at skill ranks 3, 6, 9, and 12.

entries.title: System Record Form (Ship Record Form)
entries.content:
Two pages of blank record forms:
- **Page 253**: System Record Form for recording star system details. Includes orbit rings for planet placement, with fields for planet type and orbital distance.
- **Page 254**: Ship Status Record Form. Fields include: Hull size, Maneuver rating, Ship name, Hull HP, Cruising speed, Captain's name, Starfall distance. Ship Stations table (Command, Pilot, Copilot, Sensors, Communications, Damage Control, Defenses, Engineering, Weapons) each with Location, Operator's Name, System, Skill Score. Compartments 1-12 each with S/W/M durability tracking circles.

entries.title: Supporting Cast Statistical Form (Explained)
entries.content:
A full-page annotated example of the Supporting Cast Statistical Form, using the character "Yatril Tor" (Level 1 human Diplomat/Mindwalker) as a demonstration. The form layout includes:
- Character name, species, profession, achievement level
- Ability Scores with resistance modifiers
- Durability, action check, movement rates, actions per round, reaction score, last resort points
- Perks and Flaws
- Psionic energy points, cyber tolerance score
- Attack forms with skill score, damage, and type
- Defenses (armor values for LI/HI/En)
- Complete skill list organized by Ability Score
- Gear list
- Cyber gear and mutations (if any)
- Background/behavior text description

Example character: Yatril Tor
STR 8 [+1], INT 12, DEX 11 [+1], WIL 11 [+1], CON 9, PER 11
Durability: 9/9/5/5 | Action check: 12+/11/5/2 | Move: sprint 18, run 12, walk 4 | #Actions: 2 | Reaction score: Ordinary/2
Perks: Tough as Nails, Psionic Awareness
Flaws: Bad Luck, Temper (moderate)
Psionic energy points: 6 | Cyber tolerance: 3/3/2
Attacks: Unarmed-brawl 9/4/2 d4s/d4+1s/d4+2s LI/O; 11mm ch pistol 12/6/3 d4+2w/d6+2w/d4+1m HI/O
Defenses: CF softsuit d6 (LI), d6 (HI), d6-1 (En)
Cyber gear: Cyberoptics
Mutations: Enhanced Intelligence, Wild Immune System

entries.title: Back Cover Summary
entries.content:
Back cover text summarizing the Gamemaster Guide contents:
- Rules for creating memorable supporting characters, adventures, and campaigns for any SF setting.
- A "Fast-Play" chapter that teaches the basics of moderating the game and provides a starting adventure.
- Starship construction rules and a tactical space combat system.
- Comprehensive star system design rules, including stellar classes and planetary environments.
- More than 30 alien creatures and dangerous animals to use as opponents.
- Templates that quickly bring supporting characters to life.
- Guidelines for designing new alien species and alien artifacts.
- Adventure triggers to spur your imagination and ignite the action.
- A complete index for both the Player's Handbook and the Gamemaster Guide.
